message:
perf(wal): two-pass checkpoint deduplication for sequential page writes

Rewrite the WAL checkpoint loop from a single-pass approach (which
could write the same page multiple times, with only the last write
winning) to a two-pass strategy:

Pass 1 — Deduplication: iterate WAL frames reading only headers
(not full page data), building a HashMap<PageNumber, usize> that
maps each page to its latest frame index. This ensures only the
most recent version of each page survives.

Pass 2 — Sequential writeback: sort deduplicated pages by page
number to minimize disk seeks, then read full frame data and write
each page to the database file exactly once.

Benefits:
- Eliminates redundant page writes when WAL contains multiple
  updates to the same page (common in write-heavy workloads)
- Sequential page-order writes improve I/O throughput vs the
  previous frame-order writes
- Pass 1 reads only frame headers instead of full frames,
  reducing I/O during the deduplication scan

Also adds index_seek_bug_test2 regression test module for B-tree
index seek edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
